objective: Magnify KAIZEN7 as a Universal Capability Forge for Codex, Claude Code, OpenHands and generic agents.

ranked.name: projectmem
ranked.pattern: Append-only local event log, compact agent summaries, stale-memory checks and pre-action warnings before repeated failed work.
ranked.kaizen7_adaptation: Build a local memory-governance gate that warns Codex or any agent before repeating failed fixes, touching fragile files or ignoring prior decisions.

ranked.name: Deterministic Control Plane for LLM Coding Agents
ranked.pattern: Content-addressed agent definitions, signed lockfiles, hash-chained audit logs, tiered permissions and prompt-drift detection.
ranked.kaizen7_adaptation: Treat AGENTS.md, skills, MCP configs and capability manifests as managed supply-chain inputs with hashes and explicit permission tiers.

ranked.name: Agent supply-chain risk
ranked.pattern: Agents can be tricked into installing malicious code from apparently clean repositories.
ranked.kaizen7_adaptation: Add a trust gate before clone/install/run: license, scripts, network calls, secrets exposure and human approval for untrusted setup.

ranked.name: MCP reference servers
ranked.pattern: Official reference servers for filesystems, git, memory, fetch, time and sequential thinking.
ranked.kaizen7_adaptation: Use as adapter contract examples and security baselines, not as blind production defaults.

ranked.name: Playwright
ranked.pattern: Cross-browser automation with screenshots, traces and reliable test runner.
ranked.kaizen7_adaptation: Use as the default evidence-producing browser substrate for web tasks and UI validation.

ranked.name: Code2MCP
ranked.pattern: Analyze a repository, generate an MCP service, run it, review it, fix it and produce a report.
ranked.kaizen7_adaptation: Build a local-only Capability MCP Builder that creates minimal wrappers for selected repos without deploying externally by default.

ranked.name: MCP Registry
ranked.pattern: Registry of MCP servers, acting as a discovery layer for agent tools.
ranked.kaizen7_adaptation: Provider Radar should check MCP Registry candidates before inventing a new adapter.

ranked.name: Archon
ranked.pattern: Deterministic agent workflows, Plan-Implement-Validate loops, worktree isolation and multi-agent review.
ranked.kaizen7_adaptation: Add K7 Harness DAG packets so repeated missions run as verifiable phases instead of loose prompts.

ranked.name: Codebase-Memory
ranked.pattern: Tree-Sitter code graph via MCP for structural queries, call graph traversal and impact analysis.
ranked.kaizen7_adaptation: Start with a small local code-map and promote to graph intelligence only when repo navigation becomes a bottleneck.

ranked.name: agent-sandbox
ranked.pattern: Per-agent isolated runtime for untrusted generated code, browser use, computer use and shell execution.
ranked.kaizen7_adaptation: Absorb sandbox policy manifests now; only adopt runtime when local workspace isolation is insufficient.

ranked.name: Context Mode
ranked.pattern: Store heavy tool output outside the prompt and feed agents compact references.
ranked.kaizen7_adaptation: Already adapted as lib/context-firewall.js; next step is automatic capture from forge sessions.

ranked.name: OpenHands
ranked.pattern: Generalist software-development agent with sandboxed execution and UI.
ranked.kaizen7_adaptation: Keep as a worker provider target for K7 handoffs, not as KAIZEN7 core.

next_build_order.adaptation: Build a local memory-governance gate that warns Codex or any agent before repeating failed fixes, touching fragile files or ignoring prior decisions.
